This project implements a LangGraph-based AI agent capable of performing autonomous forensic analysis on network events captured in .pcap files. Given a benchmark dataset, the agent detects vulnerabilities (e.g., CVEs), identifies affected services, and produces structured reports.
Autonomous Dwarf Fortress benchmark with DFHack environments, LLM and agent action adapters, and web dashboard.

We explore the Reflection Pattern in the context of Large Language Models (LLMs). Reflection enables AI models to analyze, evaluate, and refine their own outputs by iterating over responses, improving reasoning, and reducing hallucinations. This technique is widely used in autonomous agents, self-improving LLMs, and AI alignment.
Experimental results codes of conference paper "Towards Autonomous Deep Learning: Comparative Analysis of AI-Generated and AI-Evaluated Code Using LLMs for Image Classification"
🔍 VMS is an innovative LLM-powered agent 🤖 designed for autonomous penetration testing 🔐, evaluated through custom CTF challenges 🏆 for performance benchmarking 📊.

Tesseract : The Neural BattlefieldTessera is an AI-vs-AI simulation platform where autonomous attackers and defenders, powered by large language models (LLMs), battle in real time. Each round is orchestrated by a central intelligence layer that evaluates performance, detects breaches, and evolves the agents over time.

An autonomous multi-agent system that generates structured research summaries using LLMs and intelligent agents. Built with CrewAI, LangChain, and Ollama, it decomposes topics, retrieves and evaluates web/academic sources (via SerpAPI and arXiv API), and produces Markdown + PDF reports.

A Python-based multi-agent AI system leveraging Mistral AI’s modern LLM API to autonomously generate, evaluate, and improve solutions iteratively. Featuring executor, evaluator, and improver agents with a Streamlit frontend, it showcases an explainable, feedback-driven self-improving AI pipeline.

AI-powered tool that automates generating, evaluating, and applying code patches. It uses autonomous agents to create candidate fixes, evaluates them with LLM-driven tournaments, and applies the best patch automatically. Designed for scalability, it handles large codebases via configurable indexing and offers full experiment reporting
Benchmarking data and script used for LLM multi-agent collaboration systems from AWS Bedrock Agents Science team.

Systematic runaway-optimiser-like LLM failure modes on Biologically and Economically aligned AI safety benchmarks for LLM-s with simplified observation format. The benchmark themes include multi-objective homeostasis, (multi-objective) diminishing returns, complementary goods, sustainability, multi-agent resource sharing.

In this repository, we explore the notion of emergent misalignment in the context of tool-augmented large language models. Tested models are finetuned on partially incorrect datasets to induce misalignment, and are tested on the AgentHarm and AgentMisalignment benchmarks
HLE Proctor AI is an advanced AI agent designed to evaluate Large Language Models (LLMs) using the Humanity's Last Exam (HLE) benchmark.
This project evaluated the collective intelligence potential of collaborating machine learning models, primarily large language models, that are integrated into a mult-agent system.

This project implements an intelligent agent that utilizes large language models (LLM) to answer user queries. It integrates with a vector database (LanceDB) for document retrieval, supports web search for up-to-date information, and includes A/B testing functionality to evaluate different text-splitting and embedding configurations.

The Conversation Evaluation project uses Large Language Models (LLMs) as a judge to evaluate conversational agents using user simulation. This helps to reduce the manual effort required for evaluation.

Benchmarking whether Small Language Models can replace Large Language Models in agentic AI tasks using a co-designed router and memory system. Includes a support ticket agent, routing logic, structured outputs, and full evaluation of cost, quality, and latency tradeoffs across multiple configurations.

LLMversehub is a comprehensive resource to share practical learnings, hands-on projects, and tutorial content on Large Language Models, RAG, AI agents, MCP and AIML related topics. It serves as a central hub for developers and AI enthusiasts seeking to build, deploy, and evaluate advanced GenAI solutions in real-world applications.
